content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Addressed all three pending amendments for planned-elsewhere reconciliation correctness. Amendment 1: Split verification from commenting in verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success by restructuring into four phases — (1) verify only with post_comments=false, (2) persist verified=true records to journal, (3) post comments for verified mappings in a separate loop, (4) flush. This eliminates the crash window where a comment is posted inside verify_and_comment_mappings but the verified record has not yet been written, which previously caused duplicate comments on replay. The verified record is now durable before any comment is attempted. Amendment 2: Replaced the reconstruct_pe_mappings_from_aggregate function (which only triggered when the bead had zero mappings and only read the latest aggregate) with reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings, which always runs, scans ALL final-review StageAggregate payloads, builds a HashSet of existing identity keys (finding_summary, mapped_to_bead_id) from journal mappings, and only reconstructs PE amendments whose identity is missing. This handles the case where a bead already has older mappings from a previous restart round but a later round's mappings were lost. Amendment 3: Fixed BrCommand::comment to produce `br comments add <id> <text>` instead of the incorrect `br comment <id> <text>`, matching the actual br CLI syntax (`br comments add` is a subcommand of `br comments`). The previous command would fail at runtime, preventing mapped-to beads from ever receiving context comments.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Amendment 3: Fixed BrCommand::comment in br_process.rs to build `br comments add <id> <text>` (using Self::new("comments").arg("add").arg(id).arg(text)) instead of the incorrect `br comment <id> <text>`. Updated the doc comment to match.
2. [Completed] Amendment 1: Restructured verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success into four phases: (1) verify-only pass with post_comments=false, (2) persist verified=true records to journal immediately, (3) post comments for verified mappings in a separate loop with per-comment error handling, (4) flush. This ensures the verified record is durable before any comment is attempted, eliminating the crash window for duplicate comments.
3. [Completed] Amendment 2: Removed the !has_any_for_bead guard that prevented reconstruction when older mappings existed. Replaced reconstruct_pe_mappings_from_aggregate (single-aggregate, all-or-nothing) with reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings that builds a HashSet of existing (finding_summary, mapped_to_bead_id) keys, scans ALL final-review StageAggregate payloads, and only reconstructs PE amendments whose identity key is absent from the journal.
4. [Completed] Ran cargo test (758 passed, 0 failed), cargo clippy (no new warnings from changed files), cargo fmt --check (clean).

## Validation Evidence

- cargo test: 758 passed, 0 failed across all test suites (683 lib, 174 integration, 1 unit, 9 doc-tests ignored)
- cargo fmt --check: exit 0, no formatting issues
- cargo clippy: no new warnings from any changed file (br_process.rs, success_reconciliation.rs); all 19 warnings are pre-existing
- Amendment 1 verified: verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success now calls verify_and_comment_mappings with post_comments=false, persists verified records, then posts comments in a separate loop. The crash window between comment-posting and verified-record persistence is eliminated because verification and commenting are no longer interleaved.
- Amendment 2 verified: reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings accepts existing_mappings for dedup, iterates all final-review aggregates (not just the latest), and uses HashSet<(finding_summary, mapped_to_bead_id)> to detect and fill gaps. A bead with mappings from round N that lost round N+1 mappings will now recover them.
- Amendment 3 verified: BrCommand::comment now produces arguments ['comments', 'add', '<id>', '<text>'] which matches the br CLI's `br comments add <ID> [TEXT]...` syntax. Confirmed via `br comments add --help`.
- Files modified: src/adapters/br_process.rs (comment command syntax), src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs (split verify/comment phases, per-round reconstruction)

## Outstanding Risks

- Pre-existing clippy warnings (19 from code outside this change) cause cargo clippy -- -D warnings to fail; none are introduced by this change.
- With the split verification/commenting approach in Amendment 1, if a crash occurs after persisting verified=true but before posting the comment, the comment is permanently lost. This is an accepted tradeoff since comments are best-effort and the alternative (duplicate comments) is worse.
- The per-round reconstruction in Amendment 2 scans all payload records and all final-review aggregates on every reconciliation run. For projects with many completion rounds this adds some overhead, but payload counts are bounded by completion_rounds × stages and the reconstruction short-circuits when no missing mappings are found.
- The br_show_error_is_missing pattern for stale-bead detection is duplicated between cli/run.rs and planned_elsewhere.rs; a future consolidation could extract this into a shared utility on BrError.
